engineGuide.h2: BMW M3 Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the BMW M3 across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 2.3L Petrol
entries.history: The S14 was BMW M’s first production four-cylinder, essentially a detuned version of the Formula 1-sourced M12/13 engine. It was built specifically for Group A touring car homologation.
commonFailures: Throttle linkage wear and synchronization issues
Rocker arm failures at high rpm (track use)
Cooling system leaks (radiator and water pump)
entries.cta: Get quotes for S14B23 BMW M3 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.5L Petrol
entries.history: A rare factory stroker variant developed for the Sport Evolution (Evo 3). It featured a longer stroke crankshaft and was homologated to compete in the 2.5-litre touring car class.
commonFailures: VANOS failure (catastrophic camshaft timing)
Cylinder head cracking
High oil consumption from worn valve guides
entries.cta: Get quotes for S14B25 BMW M3 engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0L Petrol
entries.history: The first M3 straight-six, debuting VANOS variable valve timing for the intake cam. This engine shifted the M3’s character from the four-cylinder E30 to the torquier six-cylinder formula that defines the model today.
compatibleVariants: E36 M3 Coupé, E36 M3 Saloon, E36 M3 Convertible
commonFailures: Oil pump nut failure (loss of oil pressure at high rpm)
VANOS unit wear (rattle and loss of low-end power)
Head gasket failure between cylinders
entries.cta: Get quotes for S50B30 BMW M3 engine replacement

entries.title: 3.2L Petrol
entries.history: Dual VANOS (intake and exhaust) introduced, boosting power to 321 hp. One of the most charismatic inline-six engines BMW ever made, known for its individual throttle bodies and stratospheric 7,600 rpm redline .
compatibleVariants: E36 M3 Evolution, E36 M3 GT
commonFailures: VANOS hydraulic unit failure
Connecting rod bearing wear (high-mileage units)
Radiator neck cracking
entries.cta: Get quotes for S50B32 BMW M3 engine replacement

entries.title: 3.2L Petrol
entries.history: A high-revving masterpiece with individual throttle bodies and a 7,900 rpm redline. While plagued by early rod bearing issues, it won multiple International Engine of the Year awards and is the definitive M3 inline-six for many enthusiasts .
compatibleVariants: E46 M3, E46 M3 CSL, E46 M3 Competition Package, E46 M3 CS
commonFailures: VANOS failure: Rattle, loss of power, and broken oil pump disc
Rod bearing failure: Knocking noise, low oil pressure, metal flakes
Head gasket failure: Coolant loss, overheating, white smoke
entries.cta: Get quotes for S54B32 BMW M3 engine replacement

entries.title: 4.0L V8 Petrol
entries.history: BMW’s only production V8 for the M3, derived directly from the M5’s V10. With a stratospheric 8,400 rpm redline and individual throttle bodies, it offers a unique naturally aspirated character not found in later turbocharged M3s .
compatibleVariants: E90 M3 Saloon, E92 M3 Coupé, E93 M3 Convertible, E92 M3 GTS, E90 M3 CRT
commonFailures: Rod bearing failure: Knock on cold start, metal flakes in oil (failure typically 60k-100k miles)
Throttle actuator failure: Limp mode, check engine light
Idle control valve issues: Unstable idle and stalling
entries.cta: Get quotes for S65B40 BMW M3 engine replacement

entries.title: 4.4L V8 Petrol
entries.history: A stroker engine built for the limited-run E92 M3 GTS. The bore was increased and the stroke lengthened to push output to 444 bhp, creating a homologation special for the track.
commonFailures: Rod bearing failure (same S65 weakness)
High-pressure fuel pump failure
Carbon build-up on intake valves
entries.cta: Get quotes for S65B44 BMW M3 engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0L Twin-Turbo Petrol
entries.history: The first turbocharged M3 engine, featuring a closed-deck crankcase, forged internals, and twin mono-scroll turbos. Using water-to-air intercooling, it decisively moved the M3 away from high-revving naturally aspirated heritage towards torque-driven forced induction .
compatibleVariants: F80 M3, F80 M3 Competition Package, F80 M3 CS, F80 M3 Pure
commonFailures: Crank hub slip: Complete loss of timing, catastrophic engine failure
Crank hub bolt capture failure: Loss of timing (fixed with keyed hub)
Charge cooler leaks (water ingress into intake system)
entries.cta: Get quotes for S55B30 BMW M3 engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0L Twin-Turbo Petrol
entries.history: The latest evolution of BMW’s straight-six, featuring a 3D-printed cylinder head core, twin mono-scroll turbos with electric wastegates, and a flow-optimised intake. It powers the current M3 and M4 with output ranging from 473 hp to 550 hp (Competition xDrive).
compatibleVariants: G80 M3, G80 M3 Competition, G80 M3 Competition xDrive, G81 M3 Touring, G81 M3 Competition Touring
commonFailures: Oil pump failure (reported in early 2021 models)
Coolant leaks at cylinder head sealing
High-pressure fuel pump failures
entries.cta: Get quotes for S58B30 BMW M3 engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: BMW M3 Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense

problems.group: BMW M3 S65 V8 Rod Bearing Failure
problems.h4: BMW M3 S65 V8 Rod Bearing Failure - The E9x M3’s Achilles Heel
problems.affectedModels: BMW M3 E90 Saloon, E92 Coupé, E93 Convertible (2007-2013, S65B40 & S65B44)
problems.rootCause: Insufficient bearing clearance from the factory leads to oil film breakdown at high rpm. The result is metal-to-metal contact, bearing material shedding into the oil, and eventual seizure or rod failure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replacement of rod bearings with uprated aftermarket units (e.g., ACL or King), new bolts, oil flush
repairOptions.longevity: Permanent fix when done preventatively. 80k+ mile lifespan on new bearings

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Crank inspection and polishing, uprated bearing replacement, oil system flush
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if crank surface undamaged. Adds 40k-60k miles

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt engine with uprated bearings, new timing components, bench tested, 12‑month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: 100k+ mile lifespan. Only permanent solution if crank or block damaged

problems.recommendation: If your S65 is silent and below 70k miles, invest in preventative bearing replacement through a specialist. This costs £1,800-£2,800 and saves a £6,500+ replacement. If you already hear knocking, stop driving immediately. A full rebuild or replacement is usually required once the crank is scored. On a car worth £12,000-£25,000, spending £6,500-£11,000 on a rebuilt S65 with uprated bearings makes financial sense compared to scrapping the vehicle.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt S65 BMW M3 engine prices for your E9x M3

problems.group: BMW M3 S55 Crank Hub Failure
problems.h4: BMW M3 S55 Crank Hub Failure - The F80’s Fatal Flaw
problems.affectedModels: BMW M3 F80 (2014-2018, S55B30), F80 Competition, F80 CS
problems.rootCause: The factory crank hub is friction-fit only, without a positive keyway. Under high torque loads or sudden throttle changes, the hub can spin relative to the crankshaft, destroying valve timing and causing piston-to-valve contact.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Fitment of a keyed or pinned crank hub capture kit (e.g., VTT, Insane, 4Piston)
repairOptions.longevity: Permanent prevention. One-time cost before failure

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt S55 with Hub Fix
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt engine with pre-installed crank hub fix, new valves if bent, new timing components
repairOptions.longevity: 100k+ mile lifespan with the hub weakness eliminated

problems.recommendation: The S55 is a strong engine apart from the hub. If your F80 M3 is stock or tuned, a preventative crank hub fix for £1,600-£2,400 is excellent insurance. If the hub has already spun, do not restart the engine - the valves are almost certainly damaged. A rebuilt S55 with the hub fix pre-installed costs £5,500-£10,000, which is reasonable given the car’s £20,000-£35,000 market value.
problems.cta: Get quotes for BMW M3 F80 S55 rebuilt engine with crank hub fix

problems.h4: BMW M3 S54 VANOS Failure - The E46’s Weak Link
problems.rootCause: The VANOS oil pump disc hub shears or the unit’s seals degrade, causing loss of oil pressure to the variable valve timing system. This results in rattle, power loss, and eventual debris contamination of the oil system.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replacement of VANOS seals with upgraded Viton units, solenoid cleaning
repairOptions.longevity: Adds 60k-80k miles. For early rattle with no metal debris

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete VANOS unit overhaul with reinforced oil pump disc, new seals, hub replacement
repairOptions.longevity: Permanent fix for the VANOS system. 100k+ mile lifespan

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt engine with rebuilt VANOS, new rod bearings, timing chain, oil pump
repairOptions.longevity: 100k+ mile lifespan. Required if debris damaged bottom end

problems.recommendation: Catch VANOS failure early. At the first rattle, a £700-£1,200 seal replacement is cost-effective. If the hub has failed and you hear marbles in a can, stop driving. Debris often reaches the rod bearings. A rebuilt S54 with VANOS and bearing upgrades costs £4,500-£8,000. On an E46 M3 worth £15,000-£30,000 (CSL values are much higher), this is a sensible long-term investment.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt S54 BMW M3 engine prices for your E46

problems.group: BMW M3 S65 Throttle Actuator Failure
problems.h4: BMW M3 S65 Throttle Actuator Failure - The V8’s Electrical Nightmare
problems.rootCause: Small DC motors inside each throttle actuator bank overheat and fail due to degraded internal gears or worn potentiometers. The engine enters limp mode with reduced power and a failsafe warning.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replacement of the failed throttle actuator unit (BMW genuine or rebuilt)
repairOptions.longevity: 50k-80k mile lifespan. Other bank will likely fail later

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replacement of both throttle actuator units simultaneously
repairOptions.longevity: 70k-100k mile lifespan. Cheaper long-term than doing one at a time

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt engine with new actuators and uprated rod bearings
repairOptions.longevity: 100k+ mile lifespan. Only justified if bearings also failed

problems.recommendation: Actuator failure is annoying but not catastrophic - the engine does not self-destruct. Replace both units at the same time through a specialist for £1,200-£1,900; doing one bank just delays the inevitable. This is not a reason to replace the whole engine unless your S65 also has rod bearing failure. However, many owners use actuator failure as a trigger to address the bearings simultaneously, saving labour.
problems.cta: Get quotes for BMW M3 E92 S65 engine replacement with uprated bearings

problems.group: BMW M3 E46 Head Gasket Failure
problems.h4: BMW M3 E46 Head Gasket Failure - The S54’s Overheating Risk
problems.rootCause: The S54 runs high compression and generates significant heat. Over time, the composite head gasket degrades, often between cylinders 3-4 or 4-5, allowing combustion gases into the coolant system.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: New MLS head gasket, cylinder head skim and pressure test, new bolts
repairOptions.longevity: Adds 60k-80k miles. Viable if the block and head are undamaged

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt engine with new head gasket, bearings, VANOS rebuild
repairOptions.longevity: 100k+ mile lifespan. Required if head is warped or block is cracked

problems.recommendation: Head gasket failure is serious but repairable without full replacement if caught when the first symptom appears (coolant loss, misfire on startup, pressurised expansion tank). A specialist head gasket replacement costs £1,800-£2,800. However, if the engine has overheated to the point of warping the cylinder head or block, replacement is safer. Many owners combine head gasket work with VANOS and rod bearing refresh at the same time.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt S54 BMW M3 engine prices vs head gasket repair costs

problems.h4: BMW M3 E36 VANOS Failure - The S50’s Dual VANOS Trouble
problems.rootCause: The dual VANOS system (intake and exhaust) uses hydraulic pressure controlled by solenoid valves. Internal seals harden and leak, causing poor idling, loss of low-end torque, and rattling noises.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replacement of VANOS internal seals with upgraded Viton units, solenoid cleaning
repairOptions.longevity: Adds 50k-70k miles. For early symptoms without debris

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete VANOS unit rebuild including hubs, splined shafts, seals, and solenoids
Complete rebuilt engine with rebuilt VANOS, new timing chain, rod bearings
repairOptions.longevity: 100k+ mile lifespan. Required if VANOS debris damaged the bottom end

problems.recommendation: The S50B32 is a classic engine worth preserving. VANOS seal failure is almost inevitable; catch it early with a £550-£900 seal rebuild. If the car has been driven with failed VANOS for years, internal debris may have reached the oil pump and bearings. On an E36 Evo worth £12,000-£25,000, a rebuilt S50B32 at £3,500-£7,500 is a reasonable investment that preserves originality.
problems.cta: Get quotes for BMW M3 E36 S50B32 rebuilt engine with VANOS fix

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your BMW M3
finalCta.paragraph: From S65 rod bearing death rattle to S55 crank hub slip and S54 VANOS failure, every M3 generation has a known vulnerability. But these weaknesses are predictable and manageable. EngineMarket connects you with trusted UK BMW M3 specialists who supply rebuilt engines with upgraded components designed to eliminate the original failure point - not just put you back to factory spec. All units carry a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty with nationwide delivery and supply & fit options. Compare prices today and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare BMW M3 Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket’s UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific BMW M3 and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineTypes.h2: BMW M3 Engine Types - Options, Costs & What to Expect
engineTypes.intro: Every engine type for your BMW M3 is available through our UK network, with typical price ranges varying by variant, engine code (S54, S65, S55, S58) and budget.

types.title: BMW M3 Reconditioned Engines (Recon)
types.description: Factory-spec engine restored with worn parts replaced, tested to OEM tolerances.
types.frontDescription: Factory-spec engine restored with worn parts replaced, tested to OEM tolerances.
types.frontDisclaimer: Not available for all rare M3 variants.
types.backDescription: Reconditioned means your existing block is professionally restored to factory standards with new seals, rings and bearings. Only suitable for rebuildable cores.
backBullets: S54, S65, S55 blocks accepted - core charge applies
Specialist machining by BMW M engine technicians
types.cta: Compare reconditioned BMW M3 engine prices

types.title: BMW M3 Rebuilt Engines
types.description: Complete strip-down with upgraded components, often better than factory specification.
types.frontDescription: Complete strip-down with upgraded components, often better than factory specification.
types.frontDisclaimer: Includes uprated bearings or crank hub fix where applicable.
types.backDescription: A fully dismantled, inspected and reassembled engine with new pistons, rings, bearings and seals. Upgraded parts address known S65 rod bearing and S55 crank hub weaknesses.
backBullets: Includes failure-specific upgrades for your engine code
Bench tested before delivery to your UK workshop
types.cta: Get rebuilt BMW M3 engine quotes

types.title: BMW M3 Used Engines
types.description: Low-mileage salvage units tested for compression and oil pressure before sale.
types.frontDescription: Low-mileage salvage units tested for compression and oil pressure before sale.
types.frontDisclaimer: Mileage verified but service history varies.
types.backDescription: Genuine factory engines removed from accident-damaged M3s. Each unit compression tested and visually inspected for external damage. Best for low-budget repairs or donor builds.
backBullets: Typically 40k-90k miles from rear-end damaged cars
No warranty on pre-existing internal wear (e.g., rod bearings)
Fast dispatch within 2-3 working days UK-wide
types.cta: Check used BMW M3 engine availability

types.title: BMW M3 Remanufactured Engines
types.description: Core-return process with new timing components, gaskets and critical internals.
types.frontDescription: Core-return process with new timing components, gaskets and critical internals.
types.frontDisclaimer: Core charge refunded on return of rebuildable unit.
types.backDescription: Factory-certified process where your old engine is exchanged for a fully remanufactured unit. Every wear component replaced to as-new condition with updated parts where available.
backBullets: Includes S65 rod bearings upgraded to ACL spec
S55 crank hub bolt capture pre-installed
24-month warranty available on select M3 codes
types.cta: Compare remanufactured BMW M3 engine costs

types.title: BMW M3 Refurbished Engines
types.description: Cleaned, painted and tested with essential wear parts replaced. Mid-range option.
types.frontDescription: Cleaned, painted and tested with essential wear parts replaced. Mid-range option.
types.frontDisclaimer: Does not include full bottom end rebuild.
types.backDescription: A cosmetic and functional refresh - not a full rebuild. Includes new gaskets, seals, water pump and oil service. Ideal for high-mileage running engines without major internal failure.
backBullets: New valve cover gasket and oil pan seal fitted
Timing components inspected but not automatically replaced
types.cta: View refurbished BMW M3 engine options

types.title: BMW M3 Supply & Fit Engines
types.description: Fully managed supply and professional installation by UK-based M3 specialists.
types.frontDescription: Fully managed supply and professional installation by UK-based M3 specialists.
types.frontDisclaimer: Price varies by chassis (E46, E9x, F80, G80).
types.backDescription: Engine supplied and fitted by vetted specialists within our network. Includes old unit removal, new engine installation, fresh fluids and full road test before collection. engineSizes.h2: BMW M3 Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the BMW M3 is listed below, with compatible engine codes, production years, power outputs, and common failure notes - organised by fuel type.

items.title: BMW M3 2.3L Petrol Engines
items.description: The original M3 engine, developed from BMW Motorsport’s Formula 1-derived M12/13 unit. Produced specifically for the E30 M3 to meet Group A touring car homologation requirements, this four-cylinder engine defined the M3’s high-revving character and remains a favourite among classic collectors.
commonFailurePoints: rocker arm failure at high rpm
items.cta: Compare BMW M3 2.3L petrol engine prices

items.title: BMW M3 3.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The first straight-six M3 engine, introducing VANOS variable valve timing. The S50B30 powered the E36 M3 from launch, shifting the M3’s character from four-cylinder rawness to the torquier six-cylinder formula that defines the model today. Later, the S55 twin-turbo 3.0-litre redefined the M3 for the forced-induction era.
commonFailurePoints: Oil pump nut failure (S50); crank hub slip (S55)
items.cta: Compare BMW M3 3.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: BMW M3 3.2L Petrol Engines
items.description: Dual VANOS (intake and exhaust) arrived with the S50B32, boosting power to 321 hp in the E36 Evolution. The legendary S54B32 followed in the E46 M3, winning multiple International Engine of the Year awards with its 8,000 rpm redline and individual throttle bodies.
items.cta: Compare BMW M3 3.2L petrol engine prices

items.title: BMW M3 4.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: BMW’s only production V8 for the M3, derived directly from the M5’s V10. The S65B40 revs to 8,400 rpm and uses individual throttle bodies for instant response. It powers the E9x-generation M3 - the last naturally aspirated M3 before the brand moved to turbocharging.
items.cta: Compare BMW M3 4.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: BMW M3 4.4L Petrol Engines
items.description: A stroker variant of the S65, built for the limited-run E92 M3 GTS. Increased bore and longer stroke pushed output to 444 bhp, creating a homologation special for track use. Extremely rare and highly sought after by collectors.
items.cta: Compare BMW M3 4.4L petrol engine prices

engineYears.h2: BMW M3 Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This timeline covers every UK-market BMW M3 generation from 1986 to the present, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine code, variant, and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The original M3 was born from Group A touring car regulations, requiring 5,000 road-going examples. Its four-cylinder engine shared DNA with the legendary M1 supercar.
keyChanges: First M3 generation, built exclusively as a two-door coupe
S14 engine derived from BMW M1's M88 straight-six (two cylinders removed)
Evolved from 2.3L to 2.5L in final Sport Evolution models
Over 17,900 units produced - well above homologation requirements
mainEngines: S14B23 - 2.3L Petrol (E30 M3, E30 M3 Evolution)
knownFor: High-revving 10.5:1 compression four-cylinder character
Homologation special with DTM racing pedigree
Rocker arm failures at sustained high rpm (track use)
Cooling system leaks common on higher-mileage units
years.cta: Get quotes for 1986-1991 BMW M3 E30 replacement engines

years.description: The E36 M3 marked the return of the straight-six - an engine configuration that would define the M3 for decades. Single VANOS debuted on the intake cam.
keyChanges: First M3 offered as coupe, saloon (1994), and convertible (1994)
S50B30 introduced with 286hp - the first M3 straight-six since the E9 CSL
VANOS variable valve timing on intake cam only
Perfect 50:50 weight distribution with engine moved rearward
knownFor: Oil pump nut failure - loss of pressure at high rpm
Single VANOS unit wear - rattle and low-end power loss
Swirl flap issues on higher-mileage intake manifolds
Robust engine when preventative maintenance performed
years.cta: Compare 1992-1995 BMW M3 E36 3.0L engine prices

years.preview: E36 M3 Evolution: Dual VANOS Arrives
years.description: The Evolution brought dual VANOS (intake and exhaust), 3.2L displacement, and 321hp - the first M3 to exceed 100hp per litre naturally aspirated.
keyChanges: Engine upgraded to 3.2L S50B32 with 321hp
Dual VANOS (intake and exhaust cam timing)
Manual transmission upgraded from 5-speed to 6-speed
SMG I semi-automatic introduced as an option
mainEngines: S50B32 - 3.2L Petrol (E36 M3 Evolution, E36 M3 GT, 321hp)
knownFor: 321hp naturally aspirated - 100hp per litre milestone
Dual VANOS more complex and failure-prone than single
Radiator and cooling system degradation by 80k-100k miles
One of the most charismatic straight-six engines ever built

years.description: The S54B32 won multiple International Engine of the Year awards. With 343hp, an 8,000 rpm redline, and individual throttle bodies, it remains a fan favourite.
keyChanges: Sedan variant discontinued - coupe and convertible only
SMG II transmission option (Formula 1-inspired paddle shift)
M3 CSL introduced with carbon airbox and 355hp
Double-VANOS with higher compression (11.5:1)
mainEngines: S54B32 - 3.2L Petrol (E46 M3, E46 M3 CSL, 338-355hp)
knownFor: VANOS failure - broken oil pump disc, rattle, power loss
Rod bearing failure - knocking, low oil pressure (60k-100k miles)
Head gasket failure between cylinders
SMG II pump failures expensive to replace

years.ticker: E46 M3 engine replacement cost • S54B32 rebuilt engine price • E46 M3 VANOS failure fix • M3 CSL engine for sale

years.description: The only V8-powered M3, with an 8,400 rpm redline and a 4.0L S65 derived directly from the M5's V10. Still the most searched M3 for engine replacement in the UK.
keyChanges: Three body styles: E90 saloon (2007-2011), E92 coupe (2007-2013), E93 convertible (2008-2013)
7-speed M-DCT dual-clutch introduced (April 2008)
GTS variant with 4.4L stroker engine (444hp)
CRT limited edition (450hp, 67 units)
knownFor: Rod bearing failure - the #1 M3 replacement driver (60k-90k miles)
Throttle actuator failure - limp mode, both banks typically fail
High oil consumption - worn valve guides or piston rings
8,400 rpm V8 character unmatched by later turbocharged M3s

years.description: The first turbocharged M3, with S55 closed-deck straight-six producing 425hp. The coupe and convertible split to become the M4, leaving the M3 as a saloon-only model.
keyChanges: M3 becomes saloon-only (F80); coupe/convertible rebadged as M4 (F82/F83)
First forced-induction M3 engine with twin mono-scroll turbos
Competition Package offers 444hp, adaptive suspension
M3 CS limited edition (2017-2018, 453hp)

Crank bolt capture failure - fixed with aftermarket keyed hub
Charge cooler leaks - water ingress into intake system
Excellent tuning potential - 500hp+ with bolt-on mods

years.preview: Present - G80 M3: The S58 Generation
years.description: The latest M3 features the S58 straight-six - 3D-printed cylinder head core, twin turbos with electric wastegates, and up to 550hp in Competition xDrive form.
keyChanges: First M3 available with M xDrive all-wheel drive (2022 onwards)
First M3 Touring (G81) ever produced
Competition model: 503hp, xDrive adds 523hp
CS model: 543hp, limited production
knownFor: Oil pump failures reported in early 2021 models
Coolant leaks at cylinder head sealing
Massive tuning potential - 700hp+ with stock internals
First M3 to break 3.0 seconds 0-60mph (Competition xDrive)

faq.h2: BMW M3 Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: Is the BMW M3 S65 V8 rod bearing failure really that common, and can it be prevented?
items.answer: Yes, S65 rod bearing failure is well-documented across E90/E92/E93 M3s. The issue stems from excessively tight bearing clearances from the factory, which restricts oil flow when cold. Preventative replacement is highly recommended.
keyPoints: Failure typically occurs between 50,000-90,000 miles, often without warning
Cold starts and high revs before oil reaches temperature accelerate wear significantly
Uprated aftermarket bearings (ACL, King) with correct clearance eliminate the factory flaw
A spun bearing usually destroys the crankshaft, requiring full engine replacement
Specialist preventative replacement costs £1,800-£2,800 - far less than a £6,500+ rebuild
items.cta: Compare rebuilt S65 BMW M3 engine prices for your E9x M3

items.question: What is the S55 crank hub issue on the F80 M3, and does every car need the fix?
items.answer: The factory S55 crank hub is friction-fit only, not keyed. Under high torque or sudden load, the hub can spin relative to the crankshaft, destroying valve timing and bending valves. Not every car fails, but tuned cars are at much higher risk.
keyPoints: Stock cars can fail too - documented failures below 20,000 miles on unmodified engines
The hub controls camshaft timing; a slip causes piston-to-valve contact, ruining the engine
Aftermarket keyed or pinned hub kits (VTT, Insane, Mosselman) permanently fix the weakness
Preventative installation costs £1,600-£2,400 from a UK specialist
If the hub has already spun, you need a complete rebuilt S55 with new valves
items.cta: Get quotes for BMW M3 F80 S55 rebuilt engine with crank hub fix

items.question: How much does an E46 M3 S54 VANOS rebuild cost, and is it worth doing?
items.answer: S54 VANOS failure is inevitable on higher-mileage E46 M3s. A seal-only rebuild costs £700-£1,200, while a full VANOS unit overhaul with reinforced parts runs £1,500-£2,500. It is absolutely worth doing preventatively.
keyPoints: VANOS rattle and loss of low-end torque are the earliest warning signs
The factory oil pump disc hub can shear, sending debris through the engine
Upgraded Viton seals and reinforced hubs make the VANOS permanent
A failed VANOS that contaminates the oil system often destroys rod bearings too
Combined VANOS and rod bearing refresh is the smartest E46 M3 ownership move
items.cta: Compare rebuilt S54 BMW M3 engine prices with VANOS upgrades

items.question: Which BMW M3 generation has the most reliable engine?
items.answer: The G80 M3's S58 engine has proven exceptionally reliable - Auto Bild's 100,000km endurance test found almost no mechanical wear. The E36's S50 and E46's S54 are also robust when maintained, though both have known weak points.
keyPoints: G80 M3 S58: Near-flawless 62,000-mile test, no cylinder wear or leaks
E36 M3 S50: Strong engine but VANOS and oil pump nut need attention
E46 M3 S54: Reliable if VANOS and rod bearings are addressed proactively
E90 M3 S65: The least reliable due to rod bearing and throttle actuator issues
S85 and S65 V8s share the same bearing clearance flaw from the factory
items.cta: Find a reconditioned BMW M3 engine for your preferred generation

items.question: What is the correct engine oil for my BMW M3, and why does it matter for rod bearings?
items.answer: BMW M3s require 10W-60 oil - a thick, high-performance lubricant designed specifically for M engines. Using the wrong oil or skipping changes dramatically accelerates rod bearing wear, especially in S54 and S65 engines.
keyPoints: Genuine BMW M TwinPower Turbo 10W-60 is the factory-specified oil for S54, S65, and S85 engines
The thick oil protects bearings under high load and at extreme temperatures
Cold oil is viscous; always keep revs below 3,000rpm until the engine reaches operating temperature
Annual or 6,000-mile oil changes are strongly recommended for high-performance M engines
Specialist rebuilds always include an oil system flush to remove bearing debris
items.cta: Get quotes for BMW M3 engine replacement with uprated bearings

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine on a high-mileage BMW M3, or should I sell the car?
items.answer: On an E46, E90, or F80 M3 worth £12,000-£35,000, a rebuilt engine with upgraded components is almost always worth it - especially when the alternative is selling a non-running car at a massive loss.
keyPoints: A non-running M3 with engine failure sells for 30-50% of market value
Rebuilt engines include failure-specific upgrades the factory never fitted
A documented specialist rebuild adds value to discerning buyers
On a G80 M3 worth £50,000+, an engine replacement is always justified
items.cta: Compare BMW M3 engine replacement prices from UK specialists

items.question: How long does a rebuilt BMW M3 engine last, and what warranty do I get?
items.answer: A correctly rebuilt M3 engine using upgraded components should last 100,000+ miles - often longer than the factory original. All engines supplied through EngineMarket carry a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty.
keyPoints: Specialist rebuilds include uprated rod bearings, VANOS fixes, or crank hub solutions
Mr Vanos, a UK leader, offers 2 years / 20,000 miles warranty on road-use engines
A rebuilt S65 with ACL bearings addresses the factory clearance flaw permanently
S55 engines with keyed crank hubs eliminate the spun hub risk entirely
Always confirm exact warranty terms with your chosen supplier before purchase
items.cta: Get a warranty-backed rebuilt BMW M3 engine quote today

items.question: Can I fit a used BMW M3 engine from a breaker, or should I buy rebuilt?
items.answer: A used engine is cheaper (£2,000-£5,500) but carries the same factory flaws that caused the original failure. A rebuilt engine costs more but eliminates those weaknesses. For S65 and S55 engines especially, rebuilt is strongly advised.
keyPoints: Used S65 engines still have the same rod bearing clearance problem waiting to fail
Used S55 engines still have the same friction-fit crank hub weakness
A used engine has unknown service history and no warranty on internal wear
Rebuilt engines include upgraded components and bench testing before delivery
For E46 M3s, a used S54 is viable if VANOS and bearings were recently done
items.cta: Compare used vs rebuilt BMW M3 engine prices for your model

items.question: What causes the BMW M3 S54 to overheat, and when does it need a head gasket?
items.answer: The S54 runs high compression and generates significant heat. Head gasket failure typically occurs between cylinders 3-4 or 4-5, allowing combustion gases into the coolant system. Early detection prevents engine damage.
keyPoints: Coolant loss, misfire on cold start, and pressurised expansion tank are early signs
A specialist head gasket replacement costs £1,800-£2,800
If the engine has overheated badly, the head may warp - requiring full replacement
Many owners combine head gasket work with VANOS and rod bearing refresh
Upgraded MLS head gaskets are available for performance builds
items.cta: Find BMW M3 S54 engine replacement options near you

items.question: How do I know which BMW M3 engine code my car has, and why does it matter?
items.answer: Your M3's engine code is stamped on the cylinder block and listed in your V5C logbook. It matters because S54 (E46), S65 (E9x), S55 (F80), and S58 (G80) engines have completely different failure patterns, parts, and replacement costs.
keyPoints: E46 M3 = S54B32 (3.2L straight-six) - VANOS and rod bearing issues
E90/E92/E93 M3 = S65B40 (4.0L V8) - rod bearing and throttle actuator failures
F80 M3 = S55B30 (3.0L twin-turbo) - crank hub weakness
G80 M3 = S58B30 (3.0L twin-turbo) - generally reliable, early oil pump concerns
Enter your registration above to instantly identify your exact engine code
